title: Blink Charging Q1 2025 Earnings Call Highlights and Challenges
text: Blink Charging Co BLNK Q1 2025 Earnings Highlights. Key Performance Metrics. Revenue Decline: Total revenue fell sharply to $20.8 million from $37.6 million year-over-year, primarily driven by a dramatic drop in product sales from $27.5 million to $8.4 million. Service Growth: Company achieved bright spot with service revenue climbing 29.2% to $10.6 million, demonstrating strength in recurring operations. Operational Improvements: Management reduced operating expenses by 7.9% to $28.5 million and cut operating cash burn by 45% during the quarter. Major Challenges. Profitability Pressure: Loss per share widened to $0.20 from $0.17, while adjusted EBITDA deteriorated to a $15.5 million loss compared to $10.2 million previously. Cash Position: Available liquidity decreased to $42 million from $55 million, signaling accelerating cash consumption. Product Portfolio Gap: Management acknowledged that the current product portfolio does not sufficiently address the value-oriented segment of the market, limiting competitive positioning. Positive Developments. Charging service revenue expanded 35% annually. Company-owned chargers grew 22% to 7,091 units. Electricity delivered surged 66% to 50 gigawatt hours. Secured a 15-year UK contract valued at over 500,000 pounds.
